Package nonprofitbookkeeping.service

Class AccountService

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.service.AccountService
public class AccountService extends Object
Service class providing static methods for managing Account objects. This includes operations such as retrieving all accounts, calculating account balances, adding new accounts, removing accounts, and clearing the account list. Accounts are stored in a static in-memory list, making this service suitable for scenarios where a shared, application-wide account list is appropriate. For more complex applications, consider instance-based services and proper dependency injection.

    AccountService.AccountBalance
    Represents the balance of a specific account, including its ID, name, and balance amount.

  • Constructor Details

    • AccountService

      public AccountService()

  • Method Details

    • getBalanceResults

      public static List<AccountService.AccountBalance> getBalanceResults(Ledger ledger)
      Retrieves the balance for all accounts currently managed by this service. This method iterates through all stored accounts, computes their individual balances using Account.totalAccountBalance(Ledger), and returns a list of AccountService.AccountBalance records.
      Parameters:
      ledger - the ledger
      Returns:
      A List<AccountBalance> containing the ID, name, and balance for each account. Returns an empty list if no accounts are stored or available.

    • getAllAccounts

      public static List<Account> getAllAccounts()
      Retrieves all accounts currently managed by this service.
      Returns:
      A new List<Account> containing all stored accounts. Returns an empty list if no accounts are currently stored. The returned list is a shallow copy of the internal list, so modifications to the list itself (e.g., adding/removing elements) will not affect the service's internal storage. However, modifications to the Account objects within the list will affect the original objects.

    • clearAccounts

      public static void clearAccounts()
      Clears all accounts from the internal in-memory storage. This method is useful for resetting the state, for example, during testing or when loading a new set of company data.

    • addAccount

      public static void addAccount(Account account)
      Adds a new Account to the in-memory storage. The account is not added if it is null, or if its account number is null or blank. Note: This method does not check for duplicate account numbers; the underlying list will allow accounts with the same account number if added.
      Parameters:
      account - The Account object to be added.

    • removeAccount

      public static boolean removeAccount(String accountId)
      Removes an account from the in-memory storage based on its account ID (account number). No action is taken if the provided accountId is null or blank, or if the internal accounts list is not initialized.
      Parameters:
      accountId - The unique identifier (account number) of the Account to be removed.
      Returns:
      true if an account with the specified ID was found and removed; false otherwise (e.g., if accountId is invalid, or no such account exists).
